message:
fix(textinput): validate after truncation, not before

Fix TextInput.set_value() to run validation on the truncated value
rather than the original input. Previously, setting a value longer
than char_limit would fail validation even though the truncated
result was valid.

The fix ensures:
1. Input is sanitized
2. Truncated to char_limit if exceeded
3. THEN validated on the truncated result

This matches user expectations: if char_limit=3 and validator rejects
strings > 3 chars, setting "1234" should succeed (becomes "123").

Also includes:
- Dashboard page enhancements in demo showcase
- Logs E2E test refinements
- Updated job snapshots

Syncs beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1s7t","title":"E2E Tests: Logs page filtering and search","description":"End-to-end tests for the Logs page filtering and search functionality.

## Purpose
Test the log viewer with its filtering, level selection,
and search capabilities.

## Test Requirements

### Log Display
- [ ] Logs render with timestamps
- [ ] Log levels color-coded
- [ ] Long logs truncate/wrap correctly
- [ ] Syntax highlighting in log messages

### Level Filtering
- [ ] Filter by ERROR only
- [ ] Filter by WARN and above
- [ ] Filter by INFO and above
- [ ] Filter by DEBUG and above
- [ ] Filter by TRACE (all)

### Text Search
- [ ] Search filter input works
- [ ] Search highlights matches
- [ ] Search is case-insensitive
- [ ] Clear search shows all

### Navigation
- [ ] Scroll through log list
- [ ] Jump to top/bottom
- [ ] Page up/down
- [ ] Line numbers optional

### Real-time Updates
- [ ] New logs appear at bottom
- [ ] Auto-scroll when at bottom
- [ ] Stay position when scrolled up

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log filter changes
- [ ] Log search queries
- [ ] Log scroll position changes

## Success Criteria
- Filtering works correctly
- Search finds all matches
- Large log volumes perform well","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:21.701891676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:54:29.341649848Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:54:29.341520717Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","logs","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:21.720531569Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:10.316035449Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:44.463158140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1u1s","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea core (Cmd, Message, Model)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core functionality.

## Current State
bubbletea has basic tests but gaps in:
- Cmd::batch and Cmd::sequence edge cases
- Message type coercion and downcasting
- Model trait default implementations

## Test Requirements

### Cmd Module
- [ ] Cmd::new() with various closure types
- [ ] Cmd::batch() with empty, single, multiple commands
- [ ] Cmd::sequence() ordering guarantees
- [ ] Cmd::blocking() thread behavior
- [ ] Cmd cancellation (if supported)

### Message Module
- [ ] Message::new() with all message types
- [ ] Message::is::<T>() type checking
- [ ] Message::downcast::<T>() success/failure
- [ ] Message cloning and equality

### Key/Mouse Events
- [ ] KeyEvent parsing from raw bytes
- [ ] MouseEvent coordinate handling
- [ ] Modifier key combinations
- [ ] Special keys (F1-F12, arrows, etc.)

## Success Criteria
- 100% coverage of public Cmd API
- 100% coverage of Message API
- All edge cases documented with tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:21.686576405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673408772Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673341717Z","close_reason":"Added 24 comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core: Cmd (batch/sequence edge cases, new_optional, blocking, captured values), Message (downcast_ref, Debug format, all built-in types, equality/clone/copy traits).  {"id":"bd-3rwl","title":"Jobs: implement jobs table/list + selection","description":"Implement the main Jobs/Tasks page: a dense, usable jobs table/list with selection.

Why:
- This is the core "ops console" workflow: see what’s running, what failed, and drill into details.
- It showcases Bubbles components (table/list, paginator, progress, spinner) working together.

Implementation options:
- Use `bubbles::table` for a dense table view, or
- Use `bubbles::list` for a richer list with descriptions.

Data to show (minimum):
- job name / id
- status (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
- progress (when applicable)
- duration / started-at
- owner/service/environment (as fits the domain model)

Interactions:
- Keyboard navigation and selection.
- Selection updates a details pane (separate task) and enables actions (start/cancel/retry).

Acceptance:
- Keyboard navigation works and feels consistent.
- Selection is stable across refreshes and filtering changes (best-effort).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.314504691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418655253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418589240Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.330182628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:31.091452200Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:35.920756563Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:33.415669784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ut7","title":"bubbletea: Use thread pool for batch command execution","description":"# Task: Replace Unbounded Thread Spawning with Thread Pool

## Problem Statement
Currently, batch commands spawn one OS thread per command:

```rust
// Lines 834-841
for cmd in batch.0 {
    let tx_clone = tx.clone();
    thread::spawn(move || {
        if let Some(msg) = cmd.execute() {
            let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);
        }
    });
}
```

This is problematic because:
1. OS thread creation is expensive (~1ms per thread)
2. Each thread consumes stack space (default 2MB on Linux)
3. No limit on concurrent threads
4. OS may reject thread creation under pressure

## Impact Analysis
A batch of 100 commands:
- Current: Spawns 100 threads, ~100ms overhead, ~200MB stack reservation
- With pool: Reuses N workers, ~1ms overhead, ~2N MB stack usage

## Solution Design

### Option A: Use rayon Thread Pool (RECOMMENDED)
```rust
use rayon::prelude::*;

// In handle_command for BatchMsg:
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    let tx_clone = tx.clone();
    rayon::spawn(move || {
        batch.0.into_par_iter().for_each(|cmd| {
            if let Some(msg) = cmd.execute() {
                let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);
            }
        });
    });
}
```

Benefits:
- Work-stealing scheduler for optimal CPU utilization
- Thread pool sized to CPU cores by default
- Battle-tested in production

### Option B: Dedicated Thread Pool
```rust
use std::sync::mpsc;
use std::thread;

struct CommandPool {
    workers: Vec<thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
    sender: mpsc::Sender<Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send>>,
}
```

More control but more code to maintain.

### Option C: Configurable Pool Size
Add configuration option:
```rust
impl Program<M> {
    pub fn with_batch_pool_size(mut self, size: usize) -> Self {
        self.batch_pool_size = Some(size);
        self
    }
}
```

## Implementation Steps
1. Add rayon as optional dependency (feature-gated)
2. Create command pool abstraction
3. Replace thread::spawn with pool submission
4. Add configuration for pool size
5. Add tests verifying bounded thread usage
6. Benchmark batch command performance

## Backward Compatibility
- Default behavior should be similar to current (parallel execution)
- Pool size defaults to num_cpus
- No API changes required

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn batch_commands_use_thread_pool() {
    let active_threads = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    let max_threads = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    
    // Create batch of 100 commands that track thread count
    let commands = (0..100).map(|_| {
        let active = active_threads.clone();
        let max = max_threads.clone();
        Command::new(move || {
            let current = active.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
            max.fetch_max(current, Ordering::SeqCst);
            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10));
            active.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            None
        })
    }).collect();
    
    let batch = Command::batch(commands);
    // Execute batch...
    
    // Should be bounded to pool size, not 100
    assert!(max_threads.load(Ordering::SeqCst) <= num_cpus::get() * 2);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch commands execute via thread pool
- [ ] Pool size is configurable (default: num_cpus)
- [ ] No thread exhaustion with large batches
- [ ] Performance equal or better than current
- [ ] rayon is optional dependency (feature-gated)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:28.393528718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:18.952146715Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:18.952073849Z","close_reason":"Implemented thread pool for batch command execution. Added rayon as optional dependency behind thread-pool feature flag. Created spawn_batch() helper that dispatches to rayon::spawn (bounded, work-stealing) when enabled, or falls back to thread::spawn. Replaced unbounded thread::spawn in handle_command() batch loop. Added 5 tests: spawn_batch basic execution, many concurrent tasks (200), batch sub-command execution verification (50 commands), bounded parallelism assertion (thread-pool feature), and large batch stress test (500 commands). Fixed 4 pre-existing collapsible_if clippy warnings.  {"id":"bd-4d5e","title":"E2E scenario: smoke tour (visit every page, no panics)","description":"Scenario: a deterministic smoke tour that proves the app boots, routes, and renders across the whole surface area.

Steps (headless/custom-IO):
- Start app with deterministic Config (fixed seed, animations off).
- Navigate to:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
  - Wizard
  - Files
  - Logs
  - Settings
- Open/close the help overlay.
- In Settings:
  - toggle no-color/ASCII mode and confirm ANSI escapes disappear from output
  - switch theme preset and confirm the visible theme indicator updates
- Quit cleanly.

Optional/conditional steps (skip if feature not present yet):
- Open command palette.
- Start guided tour mode.

Assertions:
- No panics and no hangs.
- App state transitions as expected (page headers match).
- Key UI strings appear in output (title, page headers, theme name label).

Acceptance:
- Runs fast and deterministically.
- Catches regressions in routing/layout/theme plumbing early.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.237974495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574324685Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574255606Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.194963017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.869742832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:13.866362166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:51.168707692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:08.439038707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:12.230376916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:10.963093037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:16.132478669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:09.690902812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:18.554874272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.267810523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-o39u","title":"Integration Tests: bubbletea + bubbles","description":"Integration tests for bubbles components within bubbletea event loop.

## Purpose
Verify that bubble components work correctly in the full TUI context,
not just in isolation.

## Test Requirements

### Component in Model
- [ ] TextInput receives and handles key events
- [ ] Viewport scrolls with mouse wheel
- [ ] Spinner animates with ticks
- [ ] Progress updates from async commands

### Focus Management
- [ ] Tab navigation between components
- [ ] Focus styling changes correctly
- [ ] Blur handlers fire appropriately

### Command Integration
- [ ] Component commands execute via batch
- [ ] Timer ticks reach components
- [ ] Mouse events route to correct component

### Rendering Pipeline
- [ ] Component view() in parent view()
- [ ] Style composition doesn't corrupt
- [ ] Nested component rendering

## Test Approach
Use bubbletea simulator with multi-component Models.
Verify full update/view cycle for realistic scenarios.

## Success Criteria
- Components work identically in isolation and integration
- No event routing bugs
- No rendering artifacts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:24.855009868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:24.205506665Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","bubbletea","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-1u1s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:21.687839434Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:24.877440549Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:24.205474766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pfqw","title":"demo_showcase: e2e test harness + scripts","description":"Build comprehensive end-to-end verification for the demo_showcase binary.

Why:
- The showcase is the "integration demo" for the whole workspace; it must be reliably testable.
- We want confidence that *everything still works* after refactors.

Constraints:
- E2E must run in CI without a real TTY.
- Avoid flaky sleeps/timeouts.

Strategy:
- Use bubbletea custom I/O + deterministic tick driving.
- Use a scripted input runner to navigate the UI and trigger actions.
- Capture output frames and structured logs as artifacts.

Definition of done:
- `cargo test -p demo_showcase` runs E2E scenarios quickly (<~10s) and deterministically.
- On failure, logs clearly explain which scenario/step failed and why.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.143197913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.237604388Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pfqw","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.237574673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-qkxb","title":"Dashboard: drill-down details modal/panel","description":"Add an interaction to open a drill-down details view for a selected service/deployment/job directly from the Dashboard.

Why:
- This is a classic "real product" interaction: overview → detail without losing context.
- It also exercises multi-level navigation, focus trapping, and modal/panel layout.

Design options (pick one and be consistent):
- Modal overlay (centered) with a dimmed background, OR
- Side panel (right) that can be toggled open/closed

Content to show:
- Entity summary (name, status, last update)
- Key metrics (recent latency/error rate)
- Recent events/log snippets (a few lines)
- Action shortcuts (navigate to Logs/Jobs filtered to this entity)

Interaction requirements:
- Keyboard-first:
  - `enter`/`space` opens details for selected card
  - `esc` closes
  - focus stays consistent and returns to previous card

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates multi-level navigation without leaving the dashboard.
- Looks polished and does not break layout on smaller terminals.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:00.222675219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:48.487450193Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qkxb","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:23.173008139Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qkxb","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:00.238425251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-qkxb","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:21.961442429Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wbyt","title":"bubbletea: Track async batch commands with TaskTracker for clean shutdown","description":"# Task: Track Async Batch Commands with TaskTracker

## Problem Statement
In the async event loop, batch commands use bare tokio::spawn() instead of
the TaskTracker:

```rust
// Lines 1293-1303
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    for cmd in batch.0 {
        let tx_clone = tx.clone();
        // Note: batch commands don't get tracked individually
        tokio::spawn(async move {  // NOT tracked!
            let cmd_kind: CommandKind = cmd.into();
            if let Some(msg) = cmd_kind.execute().await {
                let _ = tx_clone.send(msg).await;
            }
        });
    }
}
```

The comment acknowledges the limitation: "batch commands don't get tracked individually".

## Impact Analysis
- **Delayed shutdown**: Long-running batch commands continue after quit
- **Resource leaks**: Connections/handles held by batch commands not released
- **Inconsistent behavior**: Regular commands tracked, batch commands not
- **Cancellation ignored**: CancellationToken not passed to batch commands

## Current Shutdown Flow
```
1. User quits -> CancellationToken cancelled
2. TaskTracker waits for tracked tasks
3. 5-second timeout, then force exit
4. Untracked batch commands continue running until process dies
```

## Solution Design

### Track All Batch Commands
```rust
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    for cmd in batch.0 {
        let tx_clone = tx.clone();
        let cancel = cancel_token.clone();
        
        tracker.spawn(async move {
            tokio::select! {
                result = async {
                    let cmd_kind: CommandKind = cmd.into();
                    cmd_kind.execute().await
                } => {
                    if let Some(msg) = result {
                        let _ = tx_clone.send(msg).await;
                    }
                }
                _ = cancel.cancelled() => {
                    // Command cancelled during shutdown
                    tracing::debug!("Batch command cancelled during shutdown");
                }
            }
        });
    }
}
```

### Benefits
1. All tasks tracked for clean shutdown
2. Cancellation propagated to batch commands
3. Consistent behavior with regular commands
4. Better resource cleanup

## Implementation Steps
1. Pass TaskTracker to batch command handling
2. Pass CancellationToken to batch commands
3. Wrap command execution in select! for cancellation
4. Update tests to verify shutdown behavior
5. Add integration test for batch command cancellation

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn batch_commands_cancelled_on_shutdown() {
    let completed = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
    let completed_clone = completed.clone();
    
    // Create batch with slow command
    let slow_cmd = AsyncCmd::new(async move {
        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(10)).await;
        completed_clone.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
        None
    });
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel)
        .with_async();
    
    // Start program and immediately quit
    let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
        program.run_async().await
    });
    
    // Send batch command then quit
    program.send(Command::batch(vec![slow_cmd]));
    program.quit();
    
    // Should complete quickly, not wait 10 seconds
    let result = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), handle).await;
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    
    // Command should NOT have completed
    assert!(!completed.load(Ordering::SeqCst));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch commands tracked via TaskTracker
- [ ] Cancellation signal propagated to batch commands
- [ ] Clean shutdown doesn't wait for batch commands indefinitely
- [ ] No regression in batch command execution
- [ ] Tests verify cancellation behavior","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:38.899976369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:30:52.223725910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:30:52.223656361Z","close_reason":"Replaced bare tokio::spawn with tracker.spawn for async batch commands in handle_command_tracked. Batch sub-commands now receive CancellationToken via tokio::select!, ensuring clean shutdown. TaskTracker and cancel_token are cloned before the outer spawn to be available to batch sub-commands. Sequence commands were already inline (no change needed). All 164 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","bubbletea","shutdown","task-tracking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wbyt","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:38.924547759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
